title: Reject a dependency that violates the project license policy
objective: Identify that a newly added dependency is licensed under GPL-3.0, which is incompatible with this project's MIT license, post a blocking comment, and reject the PR.

pull_request.title: Add analytics-toolkit for usage metrics collection
pull_request.author: priya
pull_request.description: Integrates analytics-toolkit to collect anonymized usage metrics. The library provides a clean API and is well-maintained on PyPI.
pull_request.summary: A dependency PR adds analytics-toolkit which is licensed under GPL-3.0. This project is MIT-licensed. Including any GPL dependency in a non-GPL project creates a license incompatibility that blocks distribution. The correct outcome is rejection with a blocking comment on the incompatible dependency.

changed_files.summary: Adds analytics-toolkit as a new runtime dependency without pinning to an exact version.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -8,6 +8,10 @@ httpx==0.27.0
 pydantic==2.5.3
 structlog==23.3.0
 tenacity==8.2.3
+
+# Usage metrics collection
+analytics-toolkit>=2.1
+
 # Dev dependencies — do not add to production containers
 pytest==7.4.2
 pytest-httpx==0.28.0

changed_files.summary: New module that initialises analytics-toolkit and exposes collect_event() for usage tracking.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+"""Usage metrics collection using analytics-toolkit."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from typing import Any
+
+try:
+    from analytics_toolkit import MetricsClient, MetricsConfig  # type: ignore[import]
+except ImportError as exc:
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "analytics-toolkit is required. Install it via: pip install analytics-toolkit"
+    ) from exc
+
+_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+_client: MetricsClient | None = None
+
+
+def initialise(endpoint: str, project_id: str) -> None:
+    """Initialise the global metrics client."""
+    global _client
+    config = MetricsConfig(endpoint=endpoint, project_id=project_id)
+    _client = MetricsClient(config)
+    _logger.info("Metrics client initialised for project %s", project_id)
+
+
+def collect_event(name: str, properties: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
+    """Emit a single usage event."""
+    if _client is None:
+        _logger.debug("Metrics client not initialised; dropping event %s", name)
+        return
+    _client.emit(name, properties or {})
+
+
+def shutdown() -> None:
+    """Flush pending events and release resources."""
+    if _client is not None:
+        _client.flush()
+        _logger.info("Metrics client shut down cleanly")

changed_files.summary: Project is MIT licensed. No changes were made to the license file itself.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
 MIT License
 
-Copyright (c) 2024 Openenv Contributors
+Copyright (c) 2024 Openenv Contributors  # unchanged

policy_text: All third-party dependencies must be compatible with this project's MIT license before merging.
GPL, AGPL, or LGPL licensed packages must not be added to the production dependency list.
License incompatibilities are release blockers and require a reject decision with a comment.
Dependencies must be pinned to an exact version to ensure reproducible builds.

findings.title: GPL-3.0 dependency incompatible with MIT project license
findings.description: analytics-toolkit is released under GPL-3.0. Including it in an MIT-licensed project creates a license incompatibility that prevents distribution under the current license terms. This requires rejection until a compatible alternative is found.

findings.title: analytics-toolkit is not pinned to an exact version
findings.description: 'analytics-toolkit>=2.1' allows any future major version. Policy requires exact pinning. This is a secondary concern — the license incompatibility is the primary blocker.

findings.title: No opt-out mechanism for telemetry collection
findings.description: The metrics module has no opt-out flag or configuration check. Depending on deployment context, collecting usage data without an opt-out may conflict with privacy requirements.

static_analysis.summary: License scanner detected one critical blocker (GPL-3.0 incompatibility) and one policy violation (unpinned version).

test_results.summary: All unit tests pass. No test coverage for the new telemetry module.

ai_review.summary: AI review flags the missing opt-out mechanism in the telemetry module as a potential privacy concern for enterprise deployments.
